title: scurrula longiflora
intro: Scurrula longiflora, also known as the Poison Tree or Poison Nut, is a species of flowering plant in the family Santalaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Scurrula longiflora include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a wound healing agent. Scurrula longiflora is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal use, with some herbal preparations also involving the use of its bark or roots.
